sections.title: Books
sections.description: Chapter-by-chapter notes from books on systems, engineering, and technical judgment.

entries.title: Designing Data-Intensive Applications
entries.author: Martin Kleppmann
entries.summary: Working notes on reliability, scalability, maintainability, replication, partitioning, and the tradeoffs behind data systems.

groups.title: Chapter 1: Reliable, Scalable, and Maintainable Applications
groups.summary: A vocabulary for judging systems beyond whether they simply work.

blocks.content: The chapter frames engineering quality around reliability, scalability, and maintainability. I want these notes to track not only definitions, but also where those ideas show up in real infrastructure.

items: Reliability is about continuing to work correctly when faults happen.
Scalability is a question about how load changes the shape of a system.
Maintainability depends heavily on operability, simplicity, and evolvability.

blocks.title: Question to revisit
blocks.content: What failure modes would appear first in a small trading-data ingestion pipeline: throughput, latency variance, observability, or schema drift?

groups.title: Chapter 2: Data Models and Query Languages
groups.summary: Notes on how data models shape the questions a system can answer naturally.

blocks.content: The important idea is that data models are not passive containers. They influence the abstractions, performance assumptions, and failure boundaries of the system.

SELECT symbol, max(price)
FROM ticks
WHERE ts >= now() - interval '1 minute'
GROUP BY symbol;

sections.title: Papers
sections.description: Paper notes, summaries, and implementation questions.

entries.title: MapReduce: Simplified Data Processing on Large Clusters
entries.author: Jeffrey Dean and Sanjay Ghemawat
entries.summary: A study of the programming model, runtime responsibilities, and why restricted abstractions can make distributed execution manageable.

groups.title: Core model
groups.summary: Map and reduce as a constrained interface for parallel execution.

blocks.content: The interface pushes users toward deterministic transformations while the runtime handles scheduling, locality, failures, and coordination.
map(k1, v1) -> list(k2, v2); reduce(k2, list(v2)) -> list(v2)
The useful design lesson is that a narrow API can create room for a powerful runtime.

sections.title: Technical Investigations
sections.description: Open-ended investigations, experiments, simulations, and unfinished trails.

entries.title: Limit Order Book Simulation Notes
entries.author: Krish Gondaliya
entries.summary: Notes toward a local simulation of order matching, queue priority, event replay, latency measurement, and performance tradeoffs.

items: Start with price-time priority and deterministic event replay.
Track latency per operation rather than only average throughput.
Compare map-based price levels with contiguous ladder representations.

blocks.title: Future simulation panel
blocks.content: Reserve this section for a visual replay of book depth, fills, cancellations, and queue position changes.
